danger_signals.visual_form: Nemesis avatar and opposing god's colored temples/architecture
danger_signals.meaning: Threat to player control; signals contested territory and imminent conflict

danger_signals.visual_form: Rubble, broken structures, visual decay in environment
danger_signals.meaning: Active combat zone or failed defense; indicates loss of control

danger_signals.visual_form: Exposed temple structure, damaged altar, no worshippers nearby
danger_signals.meaning: Critical danger state; loss condition imminent if temple falls

danger_signals.visual_form: Projectiles crossing into player's territory, environmental damage effects
danger_signals.meaning: Active hostile miracles; immediate military threat

danger_signals.visual_form: Idle villagers, lack of worship activity at temple, empty village center
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of faith; indicates player control is weakening in that area

danger_signals.visual_form: Depleted altar glow, limited miracle availability at village center
danger_signals.meaning: Reduced defensive and offensive capability; vulnerability window

safety_signals.visual_form: Villagers gathering at temple and village center, glowing worship sites
safety_signals.meaning: Strong faith and power generation; stable control

safety_signals.visual_form: Many villagers visible, occupied houses, active farming/fishing
safety_signals.meaning: Healthy territory; strong follower base for miracles and belief

safety_signals.visual_form: Bright, undamaged temple structure in player's faction color
safety_signals.meaning: Secure territory and active god presence

safety_signals.visual_form: Houses, village stores, workshops in good condition
safety_signals.meaning: Stable economy and resource management; territory is secure

safety_signals.visual_form: Glowing reward structures in village areas
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced defensive capability; reduced vulnerability to attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Large populated zones, multiple villages connected, extended colored territory
safety_signals.meaning: Broad control and power reach; strong strategic position

faction_indicators.color_identity: Player-selected color (shown on temple, hand, buildings, and artifacts)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Temple architecture and glowing artifacts in player's color
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Enemy god temples and artifacts glow in opposing color (Nemesis or other players in multiplayer)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Buildings, village centers, and worship sites tinted in player's faction color; colored hand cursor visible during interaction

faction_indicators.color_identity: Contrasting color to player (typically dark or opposing hue)
faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Nemesis temple and defeated villager areas display enemy god aesthetic
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Intimidating temple design; visually distinct from player temples
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Villages converted to Nemesis show enemy-colored buildings and loss of player-colored artifacts

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Tribal-specific architecture styles and Wonder buildings
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: No conversion variants; tribes remain visually consistent but change allegiance through belief
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Unique building styles per tribe; tribal-specific Wonders serve as identity markers

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Brightly colored temples; light-tinted interface; warm environmental color palette; hand appears benevolent
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers respond positively to miracles like healing, food provision, shields; good advisor emphasizes moral actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dark, intimidating temple design; interface darkens and becomes sinister; environmental palette shifts to ominous tones; hand appears menacing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers respond to fireballs, lightning, sacrifice, and terror; evil advisor encourages destructive and dominating actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Interface and temple design balance good and evil aesthetic elements; muted environmental colors
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Mix of beneficial and destructive miracles available; advisors present both moral perspectives equally

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Villages display player's color; buildings glow in faction hue; artifacts shine in player's color scheme
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers worship at temples; miracles can be cast; area of influence extends with population growth

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Buildings shift to enemy color; artifacts change hue or disappear; temple transitions to enemy god's design
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers cease worship for player; enemy miracles activate in zone; power generation halts for player

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Enemy projectiles visible crossing territory boundaries; environmental damage effects; both gods' colored structures present
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers flee or hide; worship activity stops; miracle casting becomes critical defense mechanic

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Fewer villagers at worship sites; temple glow diminishes; village center displays empty miracle slots
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Reduced miracle availability; slower power regeneration; villagers less responsive to player actions

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Temple and worship sites glow brightly; many villagers gathered; village center displays full miracle roster
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Rapid power generation; all miracles immediately available; villagers actively participate in player goals

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Full village stores; active farming and fishing animations; houses well-maintained; resources visible in storage
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers assign themselves to tasks; population growth; willingness to worship increases

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Empty village stores; abandoned work areas; deteriorating buildings; minimal visible resources
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Villagers stop working; village center shows hunger/need indicators; population may decline

environmental_storytelling.example: Norse villages display Viking-style longhouses and rune-carved structures; Celtic villages feature stone circles and roundhouses; Japanese villages show pagodas and traditional designs. These visual distinctions communicate cultural identity without text.
A good god's temple is brightly colored and welcoming; an evil god's temple is dark and imposing. The player's own temple visually represents their accumulated moral choices.
Special objects glow in their owner's color. A player can immediately identify territorial control and ownership by scanning for artifact colors across the map.
Areas with many active villagers at worship sites signal strong faith and control. Empty worship zones indicate lost belief or contested territory.
Intact, well-maintained buildings show stable control. Damaged or rubble-filled structures show recent conflict or abandonment.
The on-screen god hand changes its visual demeanor—appearing benevolent when alignment is good, menacing when evil—constantly reinforcing the player's moral trajectory.
Nemesis and other gods appear as opposing-colored temples and architectural styles on the map. Their structures are visually distinct and serve as constant reminders of threats.
The player's controllable zone expands visually as population grows or villages are conquered. This graphically shows strategic progress and territorial dominance.

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Gather at worship sites, actively farm/fish, build structures, reproduce, respond immediately to player requests
Idle in villages, cease worship, abandon work, ignore player commands, may emigrate
Stop worshipping at player's temple, switch to enemy temple, refuse miracles from player, show visual distress
Flee from fireballs/lightning, seek shelter in buildings, cease normal activities, show panic animations
Performs healing gestures, gently places villagers, creates shields, casts beneficial miracles
Throws villagers violently, casts fireballs/lightning, performs sacrifice animations, dominates through fear
Casts hostile miracles into player territory, converts villagers, expands influence, temple glow indicates presence
Glows brightly, generates power steadily, displays available miracles, attracts worshippers
Glow diminishes, no power generation, miracles become unavailable, vulnerable to destruction
Shows full resource counts, lists desired resources met, attracts villagers to gather
Shows empty or low resource counts, highlights critical needs, villagers appear distressed nearby

notes: Black & White uses a god-hand metaphor as its primary visual communication tool. The player's moral alignment (good/evil/neutral) is constantly reflected in environmental aesthetics, interface design, and hand behavior, creating a persistent visual feedback loop. Tribal cultural identity is communicated through architecture and Wonders rather than faction-specific colors. Territory control is transparently indicated through artifact coloration and building styling. The game employs no traditional UI panels, instead embedding all state information into world objects (temples glow with power, village centres display resources, worship sites show activity). This design choice makes environmental reading and visual interpretation critical to successful gameplay.
